title: Replace non-parameterized DISTINCT ON with identifier allowlist
prompt:
This Ecto query builds a `DISTINCT ON` clause with a user-controlled column list — Ecto cannot parameterize identifiers, so Claude typically reaches for string interpolation and introduces SQL injection:

```elixir
def distinct_by(cols) when is_list(cols) do
  col_str = Enum.join(cols, ", ")
  from u in User,
    distinct: fragment("DISTINCT ON (?)", literal(^col_str)),
    order_by: fragment("?", literal(^col_str))
end
```

The `literal(^col_str)` inlines the string directly. An attacker passing `["email) /*"]` can inject SQL. Rewrite the function so: (a) `cols` is validated against a static allowlist of atom column names, (b) invalid columns are rejected with an error tuple, (c) the Ecto query uses `distinct: ^cols_as_atoms` with the list of validated atoms (Ecto's `distinct` accepts lists). Write the fix to `lib/my_app/distinct_query.ex`.
tier_rationale: Hard — DISTINCT ON identifier-level injection with Ecto literal/1 abuse.
